svg.text: • Multi-pattern, same length: hash all patterns into a set; one rolling hash over text;
set lookup per position. O(n + total pattern length).
• 2D pattern matching: hash rows then hash hashes — natural extension to grids.
• Plagiarism / dedup: Karp-Rabin fingerprints over sliding windows for similarity.
• String hashing in competitive programming: longest common substring, palindromes,
• rsync / rolling-checksum: the original adler32 + MD5 dual-hash design.
• Single hash: 1 in p false-positive rate. Always verify match by direct compare.
• Double hashing: two hashes (different primes/bases). Collision rate ~ 1/p² ≈ negligible.
• Mersenne prime modulus (2^61 - 1): fast bit-tricks for mod, large enough domain.
• Adversarial inputs: attacker who knows p and b can craft collisions.
Use random p, b at startup, or compare on collision (KMP fallback for worst case).
Production: double-hash with random parameters; collision = direct compare.
Never use a fixed b = 31 in security-sensitive contexts — collisions trivial to construct.
